name: Vitoria-Gasteiz Green Ring Trail
name_original: Los bosques de Vitoria-Gasteiz — circular trailrunning por el anillo verde
description: Jog this moderate 6.2-mile (9.9 km) circular trail through the diverse forests and green spaces of Vitoria-Gasteiz's Green Ring.
region_intro: Jogging around Vitoria-Gasteiz offers access to an extensive network of green spaces and trails. The city is characterized by its mostly flat terrain and the prominent Green Belt, an ecological network encircling the urban area. This infrastructure integrates wetlands, forests, and meadows, providing diverse environments for running. Numerous urban parks and natural reserves contribute to the city's appeal for outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Vitoria-Gasteiz The most popular jogging route is Vitoria-Gasteiz Green Ring Trail, a 6.2 miles (9.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 4 minutes to complete. This route connects various green spaces, including wetlands and forests, as part of the city's Green Belt. Another top favourite among local runners is Salburua Wetland Circuit loop from Vitoria-Gasteiz, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.3 km) path. This route leads through the Salburua Park, a significant wetland complex known for its wildlife and shady avenues. Local runners also love the Salburua Wetland Circuit loop from ZEPA-ZEC Salburua BBE-KBE, an 8.0 miles (12.8 km) trail leading through the Salburua natural reserve, often completed in about 1 hour 19 minutes. Jogging around Vitoria-Gasteiz is defined by its extensive Green Belt, diverse wetlands, and natural oak woods, alongside numerous urban green spaces. The network offers options for different ability levels, from flat urban paths to routes with gentle elevation changes. The routes in Vitoria-Gasteiz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 6000 runners have used komoot to explore Vitoria-Gasteiz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: Vitoria-Gasteiz offers a wide selection of over 370 running routes, catering to various preferences. You'll find everything from easy paths to more challenging trails, with 25 easy, 227 moderate, and 127 difficult routes to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: The region is known for its mostly flat terrain, especially within the city and the extensive Green Belt. However, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5343945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaldiaran (978 m) – Eskibel village loop from Álava&lt;/a&gt; offer more significant elevation changes, reaching over 500 meters of ascent for those seeking a challenge. The city's network of 'bidegorris' (cycle paths) also provides over 135 kilometers of flat, paved routes suitable for jogging.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Vitoria-Gasteiz are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1286529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salburua Wetland Circuit loop from Vitoria-Gasteiz&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the beautiful Salburua Park.

Q: What are the best areas for a scenic run in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: The most iconic area for scenic runs is the &lt;strong&gt;Green Belt (Anillo Verde)&lt;/strong&gt;, an extensive network of parks encircling the city. Within this, &lt;strong&gt;Salburua Park&lt;/strong&gt; offers picturesque wetlands and wildlife, while &lt;strong&gt;Armentia Park&lt;/strong&gt; provides paths through natural oak woods. For urban green spaces, &lt;strong&gt;Prado Park&lt;/strong&gt; is very popular with runners, and &lt;strong&gt;Florida Park&lt;/strong&gt; offers a romantic 19th-century garden setting.

Q: Can I run with my dog on the trails in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: Vitoria-Gasteiz is generally very dog-friendly, especially in its numerous green spaces and parks. The extensive Green Belt, including parks like Salburua and Armentia, offers ample space for you and your dog to enjoy a run. Always ensure your dog is under control and check for any specific local regulations regarding leashes or restricted areas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly jogging routes in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: Absolutely. The mostly flat terrain and well-maintained paths within the Green Belt and urban parks make many routes suitable for families. Shorter, easier loops like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24677942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Walker – Parque del Prado loop from Vitoria-Gasteiz&lt;/a&gt; are excellent choices for a family outing.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the running routes?
A: Vitoria-Gasteiz's routes are rich in natural beauty. You can observe abundant wildlife, including herons and storks, in the &lt;strong&gt;Salburua Park&lt;/strong&gt; wetlands. The &lt;strong&gt;Olarizu Botanical Garden&lt;/strong&gt; showcases over 100 species of native plants. Further afield, you might encounter highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4232226" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lamioxin Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2835979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullíbarri-Gamboa Village and Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which offers 'inland beaches' and circular routes.

Q: How accessible are the running trails by public transport or car parking?
A: Vitoria-Gasteiz is well-designed for accessibility. Many parts of the Green Belt and urban parks are easily reachable by public transport from the city center. For those driving, numerous routes, especially those on the outskirts like the Salburua Park circuits, often have convenient parking options nearby, making it easy to start your run.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: The running routes in Vitoria-Gasteiz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 50 reviews. Runners often praise the extensive network of green spaces, the well-maintained paths, and the peaceful natural environment that makes jogging here a truly enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there any longer distance running options for endurance training?
A: Yes, for longer distances, the entire &lt;strong&gt;Green Belt (Anillo Verde)&lt;/strong&gt; offers a continuous network of trails ranging from 30 to 33 kilometers, perfect for endurance training. You can also combine several loops, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058972393/vitoria-gasteiz-green-ring-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vitoria-Gasteiz Green Ring Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20521106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salburua Wetland Circuit loop from ZEPA-ZEC Salburua BBE-KBE&lt;/a&gt;, to create extended runs.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: Vitoria-Gasteiz is enjoyable for running year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with blooming flowers or colorful foliage. Summers are generally mild, making early mornings or late evenings ideal. Even in winter, the city's commitment to green spaces ensures many paths remain accessible, though some higher elevation trails might require more consideration.
tips: The Armentia forest houses very diverse environments. In sections of great thickness in which maples, hawthorns, blackthorns and blackberries accompany the gall oaks, there are others of more scattered trees with large clearings, where heather, brooms and junipers grow.

This forest has a great value as a testimony of the autochthonous vegetation that centuries ago covered the area. It is similar to a small island of trees surrounded by a sea of crops, being an important refuge for wild flora and fauna in a highly humanized and ecologically simplified area.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the overall Green Ring is described as low to medium difficulty, this specific route is rated as moderate. Many sections are accessible and suitable for various fitness levels, including families and casual walkers. The extensive network of paths ensures different sections cater to different preferences and abilities.

faqs.question: Where are the best starting points and parking for this trail?
faqs.answer: The Green Ring is easily accessible from many points in Vitoria-Gasteiz. While the Virgen Blanca Square is often suggested as a general starting point for the larger Green Ring, for this specific route, you can access it near the Entrance to Armentia Forest or Zabalgana Forest. Parking is generally available in areas adjacent to the Green Ring parks, allowing quick access to nature within minutes of leaving the city center.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of mostly paved surfaces, with some sections also allowing for gravel and other accessible paths. While largely flat, particularly in areas like Salburua, there are some moderate inclines, especially in sections like the Armentia forest, which can provide opportunities for hillwork. The route connects diverse environments, including forests, wetlands, meadows, and riversides.

faqs.question: What natural features and wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: You can expect a rich and changing landscape. The route passes through dense forests like Armentia Forest and Zabalgana Forest. The wider Green Ring, which this trail is part of, includes important wetlands like Salburua, where diverse bird species, red deer, foxes, and roe deer can be observed. The trail is designed to integrate the urban environment with natural surroundings, showcasing a variety of ecosystems.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The Green Ring is enjoyable year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer is also suitable, though it's advisable to go early in the morning or late afternoon to avoid the midday heat. Winter conditions are generally mild, but some paths might be damp or muddy after rain, so appropriate footwear is recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any specific viewpoints or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers views of the city and surrounding mountains from higher points. Key natural landmarks include the Entrance to Armentia Forest and Zabalgana Forest. The Green Ring also features rest areas with benches, water fountains, and viewpoints strategically placed to appreciate the diverse ecosystems.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the Green Ring?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is an integral part of the Vitoria-Gasteiz Green Ring, which is a network of eco-recreational corridors. It intersects with sections of other official trails such as the Vuelta al Anillo Verde/Eraztun Berdearen bira, Camino de Santiago Vasco del Interior, and the Asturian Way, offering opportunities to extend your run or explore different parts of the Green Ring.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Vitoria-Gasteiz Green Ring trails are generally dog-friendly. However, it's important to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near wetlands like Salburua, to protect the natural environment and other users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to use the Green Ring trails?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and use the trails within the Anillo Verde de Vitoria-Gasteiz (Green Ring). The parks and trails are publicly accessible and designed for the enjoyment of all. The project focuses on environmental recovery and biodiversity protection, making it a free resource for the community.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like water fountains or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, along the Green Ring, you will find amenities such as rest areas with benches and water fountains. While dedicated restrooms might be less frequent directly on the trail, sections near urban areas also provide access to cafes and shops where facilities may be available.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for trail running on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain of paved and gravel paths, comfortable trail running shoes are recommended. Dress in layers suitable for the weather, as conditions can change. Bring water, especially on warmer days, although fountains are available. Sun protection, a hat, and sunglasses are also advisable. A small backpack for essentials like snacks and a phone is useful.
